metadata.title: The Americas + Sahel batch — Brazil, Mexico, Canada, Venezuela, Argentina, Chile, Colombia + the Sahel coup belt (AES: Burkina Faso/Mali/Niger) (both sides, dated)
metadata.status: leadership changes, the USMCA/tariff fights, the Venezuela election dispute + US pressure, Argentina's IMF/US support, the Lithium Triangle, and the AES/ECOWAS split are FACT (election/IMF/govt records, news of record); contested items (Venezuela 2024 result; cartel/US-strike legality) presented BOTH ways with dates; no unitary national 'mind' imputed
metadata.frame: Regional batch the user requested: the Americas (the US's hemisphere + the Latin pink-tide/right-wing swings + the resource base) and the Sahel coup belt. Threads: nearshoring + tariffs (Mexico/Canada/USMCA), the Venezuela election + US military pressure, Argentina's Milei shock + IMF/US backstop, the Lithium Triangle (Argentina/Chile/Bolivia) + Chinese investment, Brazil's BRICS role, and the Sahel's Alliance of Sahel States (AES) pivoting from France/ECOWAS to Russia. Cross-links BRICS, OPEC, IMF, US_Government, Russia, China.

the_north: USMCA + tariffs: MEXICO — a nearshoring magnet under USMCA, President Claudia Sheinbaum (Morena, inaugurated Oct 2024, first woman president), facing US tariff threats + a US push to treat cartels (Sinaloa/CJNG) as terrorist orgs amid the fentanyl crisis + concern over Chinese-EV 'backdoor' assembly. CANADA — under PM Mark Carney (2025, after Trudeau resigned), hit by 2025 US tariffs + Trump's '51st state' rhetoric, leaning on oil sands + critical minerals, retaliating on trade. grade: fact; tariff/cartel framings presented both ways.
the_bolivarian: VENEZUELA — the world's largest oil reserves but a collapsed PDVSA + mass migration; the July-2024 presidential election is DISPUTED (Maduro claimed victory; the opposition González/Machado published tally sheets claiming a landslide win) — both accounts on record. US sanctions + on-off Chevron licenses swung; in 2025 the US escalated a naval buildup + strikes on alleged drug-trafficking vessels off Venezuela (legality contested). grade: fact (the dispute + the US pressure); the 2024 result + strike legality are contested.
the_southern_cone: ARGENTINA — Javier Milei's libertarian 'chainsaw' shock (president Dec 2023): austerity crushed ~200%+ inflation but deepened recession; Argentina is the IMF's biggest debtor (~$44B+) and in 2025 got a US Treasury currency-swap/backstop (~$20B); Milei REVERSED Argentina's planned BRICS accession; Vaca Muerta shale + lithium are the growth bets. CHILE — top copper + lithium producer (Boric govt). COLOMBIA — Gustavo Petro (first leftist president), cocaine economy, 2025 US-Colombia tensions. grade: fact.
the_lithium_triangle: The Lithium Triangle (Argentina + Chile + Bolivia) holds a majority of global lithium-brine reserves — the battery-input resource that draws heavy CHINESE investment (and Western critical-minerals interest), cross-linking the EV/battery (#182) + Lobito/critical-minerals threads. State models differ: Chile/Bolivia lean state-control; Argentina (Milei) is more open. grade: fact.
the_sahel: The Sahel coup belt: BURKINA FASO (Capt. Ibrahim Traoré junta), MALI, and NIGER — after 2020-23 coups — formed the Alliance of Sahel States (AES, Sept 2024) and LEFT ECOWAS (effective 2025), EXPELLED French forces, and pivoted security to Russia (the Africa Corps, ex-Wagner). Framed by the juntas as anti-colonial sovereignty; critics cite worsening jihadist violence + lost democracy. Resources: Burkina/Mali gold, Niger uranium (a former French supply). grade: fact; 'liberation vs democratic backsliding' is the contested read.
the_honest_reading: The Americas show the US hemisphere under strain: tariff/nearshoring pressure on Mexico + Canada, a disputed Venezuela election + 2025 US military pressure, Argentina's Milei shock propped by IMF + a US swap, and the Lithium Triangle as a China-vs-West resource arena — with Brazil hedging via BRICS. The Sahel shows the opposite vector: a coup belt (AES) exiting France/ECOWAS for Russia. Contested items (Venezuela's 2024 result, US-strike legality, Sahel 'liberation vs backsliding') are presented both ways with dates; memberships/leaders are fact; no unitary national 'mind' is imputed. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.
